methodology: embed_pair 8D projection + P-vector dot product + IQR conjugation_symmetry proxy
baseline_method: IQR proxy — reproduces P1/P2/P4 but NOT P5 (see caveat)

discrimination_table.note: GUE-Poi separation NEGATIVE (-5.5 pt): Poisson > GUE on this metric. P5 is not a discriminant via embed_pair.

key_findings.P5_proxy_caveat: IQR proxy gives 89.7% for P5 vs known 64.6% — proxy fails for zero-straddling sequences
key_findings.variance_discrimination: All P-vectors show Poi/GUE variance ratio 3-5x — consistent level-repulsion signal
key_findings.actual_zeros_tighter_than_GUE: Act/GUE variance ≈ 0.63 across all P-vectors — actual RH zeros tighter than Wigner
key_findings.P5_GUE_Poi_discrimination_proxy: Only 2 pt separation (GUE-Poi) on P5 using proxy — weak via IQR metric
key_findings.conclusion: Variance ratio (3-5x) confirms GUE/Poisson discrimination. P5 anomaly in Phase 9A actual values needs CAILculator replication with same 98-gap dataset to confirm GUE ~52%, Poisson ~78% baselines.

p5_followup.methodology_note: Metric: fraction within mean±1std. Calibration: actual proxy=63.9% matches known 64.6% (Δ=0.7%).
p5_followup.verdict: WEAK/NO special discrimination on embed_pair P5 (-5.5 pt, below 5 pt threshold)
p5_followup.key_finding: Ordering is Actual(64%)≈GUE(68%)<Poisson(73%) — GUE is NOT lower than Poisson on P5 via embed_pair. Phase 9A prediction (GUE~52%<Poisson~78%) does not replicate with embed_pair projection.
p5_followup.ordering_note: Actual clusters with GUE (Δ=3.1 pt) confirming actual zeros have GUE-like properties, but P5 is NOT a discriminant when using embed_pair transformation.
p5_followup.phase9a_discrepancy: Phase 9A used direct gap sequences; Phase 10C uses embed_pair 8D projection. The transformation changes the discrimination signal direction on P5.
